Because the post is primarily:

challenging a belief

column1: "The pieces around him are a threat too giving Vrunson more room to play his game."

column1: "They’re comparable enough. The Spurs have been challenged these playoffs and the Knicks have no answer for Wemby. The Knicks might be the 3rd best team San Antonio will face these playoffs"
column3: Provides matchup-based reasoning and playoff context before reaching a conclusion.

column1: "On one hand the Knicks are one of the hottest teams in the playoffs ever. On the other hand the Knicks were a very mediocre/good team in a weak conference and were down 1-2 to the Hawks. IMO while their streak is impressive it also came against teams that weren't great and then completely capitulated in the later games. Its likelier the Knicks are still that mediocre/good team rather some all time great team. I thought the Knicks would make the finals and argued with people that thought they would lose to Philly or Cleveland. So I dont have any hate toward the Knicks or what they have done. We have seen teams get hot in the playoffs and then flame out. Cavaliers did that in the seasons they lost to the warriors. The Thunder were 8-0 heading into the WCF and lost to us. The Spurs back in 2012 were red hot and started off 10-0 in the playoffs only to get back door swept by the Thunder. I think the Spurs win the series but hard to say how many games. I keep thinking 5 games but it doesnt really matter the amount. I think the Spurs handle the Knicks in San Antonio and all the momentum/hype they currently have will be gone by the weekend. I felt confident the Spurs would get past the Thunder and feel even more confident they arent going to lose a 7 game series to the Knicks when they have home court and have played better basketball through the entirety of the season."
column3: Most of the comment explains why playoff momentum may be misleading using examples, comparisons, and historical precedent.

column1: "knicks are mediocre/good team in a weak conference. This is just straight up bias and wrong. Im a GSW fan but I live in NY so I've always liked the knicks also, and these last three years the teams improved significantly. Theyre a great team and they got here based on their skill. That said im not sure how it'll go. I dont expect a sweep in either direction but I also dont think its going to go 4-3 either. All I care about is good games and a good series."
column3: Rebuts a claim by explaining team improvement and attributing success to performance rather than conference weakness.

column1: "They have improved but it doesn't change the fact that the conference is weak. The teams they beat would be borderline playoff teams in the west. Up until they turned it around the against the hawks down 2-1 they were very mediocre/good for the season but not great by any means. Also it's not their fault the conference sucks. I fully expected them to wipe the floor of Philly and especially Cleveland because those teams are just completely mediocre. I don't think their 10 game stretch undoes who they were the other 85 games they played. No one was saying the Knicks were a great team, heck a lot of people in NY were giving up on them through the season and against the hawks and I know this because I follow the Knicks because all my other teams are NY (Jets/Mets)"
column3: Uses conference comparisons, sample-size reasoning, and season-long performance to support the conclusion.

column1: "You beat the thunder in 7 games without 2/3 strongest offensive players. Your route to the finals was no stronger or weaker than the Knicks. The 76ers beat the Celtics and the Cavs beat the pistons, and the Knicks swept both of them. The Knicks have had the spurs number for the past two seasons, dropping what, 1-2 games over the last two years? Spurs are not winning in 5, this is a toss up and it entirely depends on if Wemby is truly good enough to 1v5 the Knicks, because there isn’t a single other player on the spurs who are better than the Knicks players"
column3: Provides multiple supporting points about injuries, playoff paths, and roster strength before reaching a prediction.

column1: "The Thunder were 39-10 and 8-0 without Williams. He was barely a part of their season. The Spurs also didnt have Fox for games 1 or 2. The 76ers and Cavaliers would be borderline playoff teams in the West. You cant be serious thinking they are of the same level of the teams the Spurs played. Who cares about regular season games? Wemby played 25 minutes in half those games. Hardly true, Castle, Harper, Fox are not good? I mean come on. Spurs in 5 and if the Knicks are lucky Spurs in 6 or 7 Check back when its done"
column3: Uses statistics, injury context, and matchup arguments to support a conclusion.

column1: "I know that looking at regular season matchups can be a fool's errand, but it's interesting to look back on this matchup in the regular season. In a narrow win and the Cup loss, Wemby only played 24 minutes. But then the Spurs also got blown out in March when he played 34 minutes: Spurs vs Knicks, March 1, 2026 | Basketball-Reference.com In the losses, the Spurs lost the rebounding battle by double digits. In the game the Spurs won, Mitchell Robinson did not play. I think he's the X-factor here, and the Spurs will likely intentionally foul him to try to get him out of the game and off the glass. We'll see how Mike Brown handles that. If his finger injury makes his FT shooting somehow even worse that will make things interesting."
column3: Uses statistics, matchup evidence, and strategic reasoning to explain a potential series factor.

column1: "I think you should avoid bandwagoning on a team like the Spurs or Knicks because part of the joy of sports fandom you're missing out on is watching young guys and getting excited and having hope and having it actually pay off. If you want a team that's probably about to start trying to win, maybe the Jazz? Or if you want a team that's closer to actually winning, the Pistons?"
column3: Gives reasons for the recommendation and explains the logic behind alternative choices.

column1: "People want to immediately crown players the GOAT these days. I don’t get it, and have been saying for weeks that we should wait until he’s won an MVP, finals, etc before you even start mentioning it. He’s got a long way to go before he can be mentioned in the same breath as Jordan and LeBron, even if he certainly has the potential to be that good one day."
column3: Evaluative claim about player greatness trajectory; reasoning supports opinion.

column1: "Wemby screwed that up. He needs to make eye contact with his teammate before making that pass. But what killed them was the 8 missed free throws at home. There’s one silver lining in this game for a team with silver and black. Spurs did claw back from being 14 down. There was a moment in the game where even the Knicks fans were thinking: “Uh, oh. My Knicks are doing Knicks things again. They’re choking. They’re blowing it like the Knicks usually do. The Knicks are still the same old Knicks doing the same old Knicks things.” A disaster waiting to happen like the Dallas Cowboys. If you’re a Spurs fan, don’t give up. Spurs are finally discovering how to beat the Knicks and did outscore them those last 5 minutes. The Knicks could not score for a good 3.5 minutes. If Spurs can keep exploiting it, they still got a chance. Focus winning Game 3 and start from there. Mitch Johnson needs to make adjustments as often as he can. He cannot play his team the same way over and over. He needs to change things up to make the opponent readjust their own game plan. Don’t give up though, Spurs fans. Win at least one game these next two if you can’t win both. Then I believe a 3-1 comeback is possible when Game 5 and 7 are held at San Antonio. It’s not over. Play these Knicks like those last 5 minutes in Game 2 and make sure to make your free throws. Prove to the world that the Knicks are still the same old Knicks."
column3: Contains tactical reasoning, game breakdown, and adjustment logic despite opinionated tone.

column1: "He’s 22 relax. I say this as a Knicks fan praying for his downfall this series."

column1: "Fair enough. There were a lot of great teams and great players who never won it all. Winning a championship is difficult. Karl Malone, Iverson, Nash, harden and so on were all great players who had great teams but ended up facing an even greater team or were just plain unlucky. It is what it is and yeah fair point"
column3: Explains why great players may not win championships using contextual comparisons.

column1: "In many of these cases of ringless all time greats, it was because they either wound up in the middle of a dynasty (80s Lakers/Celtics, 90s Bulls, 2000s Lakers/Spurs, or the 2010s Cavs/Warriors) or they don’t have a good team built around them. There currently isn’t a ‘dynasty’. Wemby has a great team built around him. The Spurs are ahead of schedule by even making it to the finals and if they win that’s amazing. But let’s not forget that Wemby is in his 3rd year, Castle is in his 2nd, and Harper is a rookie. The Knicks happen to have a great matchup against the Spurs as well. To say that the young Spurs core who made the finals all before the age of 25 will never win a ring is a bit absurd."
column3: Structured explanation using dynasty context, roster construction, and comparison.

column1: "All these panic posts about a 22 year old In their first playoffs. Not just playoffs. Nba finals. It's hilarious. Go find the list of people that made it to the finals at that same age or younger as the #1 option and get back to me."

column1: "This take is so hot that it should go to hell and burn there forever. I wholeheartedly believe that Wemby has been getting glazed too much and has prolly gotten overrated this season, but saying he's going to end up as a perennial choker should get you banned from any basketball take. His ceiling is Lebron/MJ level and he's shown flashes of that. Regardless of what you want to think, he's the main reason his team reached the finals. Unless he pulls a Ben Simmons, there's no way he's not going to win a single ring."

“Here are my unpopular opinions/predictions. If you want reasoning on any of them, I'd be happy to provid.

Kobe is top 3 all time. Could've been the GOAT if it wasn't for his mentality early on in his career.

Warriors were just by far the best team last season and were incredibly unlucky.

Kawhi Leonard wasn't overly great in the finals and did not deserve finals MVP.

Cavs lucked into a finals win in 2016 with the help from the league. In saying that, if it wasn't for injuries in 2015, they would've won so it all evens out.

Steph is the best player in the league currently with Harden being second or third best alongside KD.

LeBron wasn't the most important player on the lakers last season, Lonzo was.

Sixers had a bad off-season and will only look better as the conference got significantly worse around them.

Neither LA team is winning this year. A team with a loyal star will. (Rockets, Warriors, Trailblazers or Bucks).

Kuzma was right. Melo is better than a lot of the league but the reason he doesn't have a team is not because of his talent, but rather the fact he won't take a back seat. If he did, he would be a great bench piece.

Lonzo Ball is an incredible talent and barring injury, will be a top 10 player in the league.

Buddy Hield has an incredible work ethic and will lead the kings to the playoffs this year. Yes he is 26 but he picked up the game late and has a lot of room to learn.

The Kings will win a title before the 76ers.

Rubio will have an incredible year next to Booker and having Ayton to feed. Suns will be much better.

Knicks finally had a good off-season. They finally have direction which is to rebuild and have 3/5 positions already set for the future and potentially 4/5 depending on how Frank and DSJ progress.

Even though MJ is the GOAT, he is probably the most overrated player of all time. LeBron is so close to him and honestly, he had the luckiest career ever and everything that happened to him was so favourable and got good calls constantly in the playoffs. He shouldn't be seen as the GOAT sportstar as Gretzky has him beat in the American sports and Don Bradman is the best internationally.”

“Steph Curry is the most over-rated player in NBA history.

The only reason he has a ring is because the first time every team he played had their starting PG injured.

Jrue Holiday

Mike Conley

Pat Beverley

Kyrie

Next year they lost to a fully healthy Cavs team.

Won the next 2 because they were stacked with KD.

Good player but not great.”
column3: Causal argument about injuries and roster strength

column1:
“Windy said on his podcast that he did a segment of 5 "realistic" trades for Giannis on First Take. He spoke about how much backlash he got on it, but that the 5 ideas were not his ideas of a good trade but ones that ESPN told him to push. It was all Lakers, Golden State, Knicks, Clippers, and I think Houston.

Just fun to see Windy slightly reveal what we have been seeing for years. The talking heads get pressured from ESPN to speak these things into reality, stars forcing themselves to big markets.

Also, fuck Perk”
column3: Explains media incentive structure and cause-effect (ESPN pressure → narratives)
